main_paper.raw: {"methods":["ExVideo: a parameter-efficient post-tuning methodology for video synthesis models to extend video length","Design of extension strategies for temporal model architectures: 3D convolution, temporal attention, and positional embedding","Adapter components for temporal module enhancement in video diffusion models","Post-tuning applied to Stable Video Diffusion to create ExSVD"],"problems":["Limited video length in current video diffusion models due to computational constraints","Existing methods for generating longer videos either require expensive retraining, reduce coherence, or do not extend narrative duration","Need for extending video duration without compromising quality or generalization"],"datasets":["A dataset comprising 40,000 videos (details not specified)"],"metrics":["Frame length (number of frames generated)","Training cost (GPU hours)","Video quality (coherence, generalization, visual quality)"],"results":[{"metric":"Maximum video length (frames)","value":"128 frames (5× original 25 frames)"},{"metric":"Training cost","value":"1.5k GPU hours"},{"metric":"Dataset size","value":"40k videos"}],"novelty_claims":["First parameter-efficient post-tuning method (ExVideo) to extend the temporal scale of existing video diffusion models for long video generation","ExVideo enables extension of video length up to 5× original capacity (from 25 to 128 frames) without compromising generative quality or generalization","Adapter-based extension strategies for various temporal architectures (3D convolution, temporal attention, positional embedding)","Demonstrates that post-tuning can efficiently enhance video synthesis models for long video generation with limited computational resources"]}

methods: ExVideo: a parameter-efficient post-tuning methodology for video synthesis models to extend video length

Adapter components for temporal module enhancement in video diffusion models

novelty_claims: First parameter-efficient post-tuning method (ExVideo) to extend the temporal scale of existing video diffusion models for long video generation

methods: FusionFrames: a two-stage latent diffusion text-to-video generation pipeline based on a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model (Kandinsky 3.0)
Keyframe generation using separate temporal blocks for temporal conditioning (compared to mixed spatial-temporal layers)
Efficient interpolation model that predicts groups of interpolated frames together to improve speed and quality
MoVQ-GAN-based video decoding scheme with various architectural configurations
problems: High computational cost of training and inference in text-to-video (T2V) generation

results.value: >3x faster than other masked frame interpolation architectures

novelty_claims: Proposes FusionFrames, a two-stage latent diffusion T2V pipeline leveraging a pretrained T2I model (Kandinsky 3.0) with explicit separation of keyframe and interpolation stages.
Introduces the use of separate temporal blocks for temporal conditioning in keyframe generation, showing superior performance over mixed spatial-temporal layers.
Presents an efficient interpolation architecture that predicts groups of frames together, significantly reducing inference time and improving frame fidelity.
Comprehensively analyzes and optimizes MoVQ-GAN-based video decoder architectures for improved consistency and quality with fewer parameters.
Achieves top-2 overall and top-1 among open-source solutions on CLIPSIM and FVD metrics for text-to-video generation.

methods: Content-motion latent diffusion model (CMD)
Autoencoder for decomposing video into content frame and motion latent representation

Lightweight diffusion model for motion latent generation conditioned on content frame
problems: High memory and computational requirements of video diffusion models due to high-dimensional video data

novelty_claims: Proposes CMD, a novel latent video diffusion model that decomposes videos into a content frame and a low-dimensional motion latent, enabling efficient video generation.

CMD achieves significantly better video generation quality and efficiency (memory, computation, and speed) compared to prior state-of-the-art methods.

methods: Disentangling motion from appearance at the data level by using low-quality videos for motion and high-quality images for appearance/concept composition

Finetuning only the spatial modules of a fully trained video model with synthesized high-quality images (rather than using LORA or finetuning both modules)

Providing a practical pipeline for the community to train high-quality video models using only low-quality videos and high-quality images

results.value: Eliminated at high resolutions using re-dilation (qualitative improvement over baselines)

novelty_claims: Identifies limited convolutional receptive field as the primary cause of object repetition in high-resolution generation, rather than attention token quantity.
Proposes a tuning-free, dynamic re-dilation strategy for convolutional layers during inference to solve repetition and structure issues.
Introduces dispersed convolution and noise-damped classifier-free guidance for ultra-high-resolution image generation (e.g., 4096x4096).
Demonstrates that pre-trained diffusion models can be directly used for high-resolution and arbitrary aspect ratio image generation without any retraining or optimization.
Achieves state-of-the-art performance in high-resolution image synthesis, especially in texture details, without additional training.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Cascaded video latent diffusion models (base T2V model, temporal interpolation model, video super-resolution model)","Incorporation of temporal self-attention with rotary positional encoding (RoPE)","Joint image-video fine-tuning strategy","Leveraging a pre-trained text-to-image (T2I) model (Stable Diffusion) as the foundation for T2V generation"],"problems":["High-quality text-to-video (T2V) generation that is visually realistic and temporally coherent","Preserving creative generation capabilities from pre-trained T2I models in T2V synthesis","Efficient training of T2V models with limited computational resources","Lack of high-quality, diverse, and high-resolution text-video datasets for T2V training"],"datasets":["Vimeo25M (introduced in this work)","WebVid10M (used for comparison)"],"metrics":["Video quality (quantitative and qualitative)","Diversity","Aesthetic appeal"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes LaVie, an integrated video generation framework using cascaded latent diffusion models for high-quality T2V generation.","Demonstrates that simple temporal self-attention with RoPE is sufficient for capturing temporal correlations in video data.","Shows that joint image-video fine-tuning is crucial for maintaining creativity and knowledge transfer from T2I to T2V models.","Introduces Vimeo25M, a new large-scale, high-resolution, and diverse text-video dataset for T2V training.","Achieves state-of-the-art performance in T2V generation both quantitatively and qualitatively."]}

methods: Cascaded video latent diffusion models (base T2V model, temporal interpolation model, video super-resolution model)

Joint image-video fine-tuning strategy
Leveraging a pre-trained text-to-image (T2I) model (Stable Diffusion) as the foundation for T2V generation

Demonstrates that simple temporal self-attention with RoPE is sufficient for capturing temporal correlations in video data.
Shows that joint image-video fine-tuning is crucial for maintaining creativity and knowledge transfer from T2I to T2V models.
Introduces Vimeo25M, a new large-scale, high-resolution, and diverse text-video dataset for T2V training.
Achieves state-of-the-art performance in T2V generation both quantitatively and qualitatively.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Latent-Shift: a parameter-free temporal shift module for latent diffusion-based text-to-video generation","Shifting portions of feature map channels forward and backward along the temporal dimension in the U-Net architecture"],"problems":["Efficient text-to-video (T2V) generation using pretrained text-to-image (T2I) models","Reducing model complexity and computational cost in T2V generation","Achieving temporal coherence in video generation without adding extra parameters"],"datasets":["MSRVTT","UCF-101"],"metrics":["User study (human evaluation)","Standard video generation benchmarks (not specified in detail)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a parameter-free temporal shift module for extending T2I latent diffusion models to T2V generation without adding new parameters.","Latent-Shift enables the use of a spatial U-Net for video generation by shifting feature map channels temporally, allowing motion learning without extra parameters.","Latent-Shift can generate both videos and images despite being finetuned for T2V generation, a unique capability.","Latent-Shift achieves comparable or better results than prior latent video diffusion models with temporal attention, while being more efficient and having fewer parameters."]}

novelty_claims: Proposes a parameter-free temporal shift module for extending T2I latent diffusion models to T2V generation without adding new parameters.
Latent-Shift enables the use of a spatial U-Net for video generation by shifting feature map channels temporally, allowing motion learning without extra parameters.
Latent-Shift can generate both videos and images despite being finetuned for T2V generation, a unique capability.
Latent-Shift achieves comparable or better results than prior latent video diffusion models with temporal attention, while being more efficient and having fewer parameters.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["StreamDiffusion pipeline","Stream Batch (batching denoising process)","Input-output queue for parallel streaming","Residual Classifier-Free Guidance (RCFG)","Stochastic Similarity Filter (SSF)"],"problems":["Enabling real-time interactive image generation with diffusion models","Improving throughput and reducing latency for continuous input scenarios (e.g., Metaverse, live video streaming, broadcasting)","Reducing redundant computation in classifier-free guidance","Reducing energy consumption of diffusion pipelines"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Frames per second (fps)","Speedup (x)","Energy consumption reduction (x)"],"results":[{"metric":"Frames per second (fps) on RTX4090","value":"91.07"},{"metric":"Speedup over sequential denoising (Stream Batch)","value":"1.5x"},{"metric":"Speedup over conventional CFG (RCFG)","value":"2.05x"},{"metric":"Throughput improvement over Diffusers AutoPipeline","value":"59.56x"},{"metric":"Energy consumption reduction on RTX3060","value":"2.39x"},{"metric":"Energy consumption reduction on RTX4090","value":"1.99x"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a pipeline-level solution (StreamDiffusion) for real-time, high-throughput interactive image generation with diffusion models.","Introduces Stream Batch, transforming sequential denoising into a batched process for higher throughput.","Designs a novel input-output queue to parallelize streaming and handle input/model frequency mismatch.","Proposes Residual Classifier-Free Guidance (RCFG) to reduce redundant U-Net computations in classifier-free guidance, requiring only one or zero negative conditional denoising steps.","Introduces a stochastic similarity filter (SSF) to optimize power consumption by filtering redundant computations."]}

methods: StreamDiffusion pipeline
Stream Batch (batching denoising process)
Input-output queue for parallel streaming
Residual Classifier-Free Guidance (RCFG)
Stochastic Similarity Filter (SSF)
problems: Enabling real-time interactive image generation with diffusion models

novelty_claims: Proposes a pipeline-level solution (StreamDiffusion) for real-time, high-throughput interactive image generation with diffusion models.
Introduces Stream Batch, transforming sequential denoising into a batched process for higher throughput.
Designs a novel input-output queue to parallelize streaming and handle input/model frequency mismatch.
Proposes Residual Classifier-Free Guidance (RCFG) to reduce redundant U-Net computations in classifier-free guidance, requiring only one or zero negative conditional denoising steps.
Introduces a stochastic similarity filter (SSF) to optimize power consumption by filtering redundant computations.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["T2V-Turbo: a text-to-video model that integrates feedback from a mixture of differentiable reward models (image-text and video-text reward models) into the consistency distillation process of a pre-trained T2V model","Direct reward optimization on single-step generations during consistency distillation, bypassing memory constraints of backpropagation through iterative sampling","Distillation of T2V-Turbo from existing T2V models (VideoCrafter2 and ModelScopeT2V)"],"problems":["Slow sampling speed of diffusion-based text-to-video models due to iterative sampling","Quality bottleneck in video consistency models (VCMs) when reducing inference steps for faster generation","Difficulty in aligning generated videos with human preferences, especially in temporal dynamics and transitions","High memory cost of backpropagating reward gradients through iterative sampling processes"],"datasets":["VBench (video evaluation benchmark)","EvalCrafter (700 prompts for human evaluation)","WebVid-10M (used for training base models)"],"metrics":["VBench total score","Human preference (human evaluation)"],"results":[{"metric":"VBench total score (4-step T2V-Turbo)","value":"Highest score, surpassing Gen-2 and Pika (exact value not specified)"},{"metric":"Human preference (EvalCrafter, 700 prompts)","value":"4-step T2V-Turbo generations preferred over 50-step DDIM teacher model generations"}],"novelty_claims":["First to learn a T2V model with feedback from a mixture of reward models, including a video-text model, for both spatial and temporal alignment with human preferences","Establishes new state-of-the-art on VBench with only 4 inference steps, outperforming proprietary models trained with more resources","Achieves over tenfold inference acceleration (4 steps vs. 50 steps) while improving video generation quality, as validated by human evaluation"]}

problems: Slow sampling speed of diffusion-based text-to-video models due to iterative sampling

results.value: Highest score, surpassing Gen-2 and Pika (exact value not specified)
4-step T2V-Turbo generations preferred over 50-step DDIM teacher model generations

novelty_claims: First to learn a T2V model with feedback from a mixture of reward models, including a video-text model, for both spatial and temporal alignment with human preferences
Establishes new state-of-the-art on VBench with only 4 inference steps, outperforming proprietary models trained with more resources
Achieves over tenfold inference acceleration (4 steps vs. 50 steps) while improving video generation quality, as validated by human evaluation

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["AnimateDiff: a plug-and-play motion module for animating personalized text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models","Three-stage training strategy for the motion module: domain adapter fine-tuning, motion module training with fixed base T2I and domain adapter, and MotionLoRA adaptation","MotionLoRA: a lightweight fine-tuning technique using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to adapt the motion module to new motion patterns","Integration of the motion module with existing content-controlling approaches such as ControlNet"],"problems":["Enabling animation generation from personalized T2I diffusion models without requiring model-specific fine-tuning","Preserving visual quality and domain knowledge of personalized T2I models while adding motion dynamics","Reducing computational and data collection costs for animating personalized T2I models"],"datasets":["WebVid-10M","Personalized T2I models from Civitai","Personalized T2I models from Hugging Face"],"metrics":["Temporal smoothness","Visual quality","Motion diversity"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First practical pipeline (AnimateDiff) to animate any personalized T2I model without model-specific fine-tuning","Plug-and-play motion module trained once and transferable to any personalized T2I model from the same base","MotionLoRA: lightweight fine-tuning for adapting motion modules to new motion patterns with as few as 50 reference videos and minimal storage overhead (~30M)","Demonstrated compatibility with content-controlling approaches (e.g., ControlNet) for controllable animation generation","Comprehensive evaluation on a wide range of community models and comparison with both academic and commercial baselines"]}

methods: AnimateDiff: a plug-and-play motion module for animating personalized text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models

MotionLoRA: a lightweight fine-tuning technique using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to adapt the motion module to new motion patterns
Integration of the motion module with existing content-controlling approaches such as ControlNet
problems: Enabling animation generation from personalized T2I diffusion models without requiring model-specific fine-tuning
Preserving visual quality and domain knowledge of personalized T2I models while adding motion dynamics
Reducing computational and data collection costs for animating personalized T2I models
datasets: Personalized T2I models from Hugging Face
novelty_claims: First practical pipeline (AnimateDiff) to animate any personalized T2I model without model-specific fine-tuning
Plug-and-play motion module trained once and transferable to any personalized T2I model from the same base
MotionLoRA: lightweight fine-tuning for adapting motion modules to new motion patterns with as few as 50 reference videos and minimal storage overhead (~30M)

Comprehensive evaluation on a wide range of community models and comparison with both academic and commercial baselines

Introduces a progressive refinement architecture where each decoder only needs to update intermediate optical flow, occlusion merge mask, and image residual, simplifying the synthesis task.
Utilizes existing texture from adjacent input frames instead of directly synthesizing from deep features, reducing computation.
Introduces a new annealed multi-scale reconstruction loss that does not require a pre-trained optical flow network, improving training and inference efficiency.
Achieves significant reduction in model size and running time compared to state-of-the-art methods while maintaining favorable quantitative and qualitative results.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Improved diffusion model architecture via ablation studies","Classifier guidance for conditional image synthesis (using classifier gradients to guide diffusion sampling)","Tuning classifier gradient scale to trade off diversity and fidelity","Combining classifier guidance with upsampling diffusion models"],"problems":["Achieving state-of-the-art image sample quality in unconditional and conditional image synthesis","Bridging the gap between diffusion models and GANs in terms of sample quality and distribution coverage","Improving the scalability and training stability of generative models for image synthesis"],"datasets":["ImageNet 128x128","ImageNet 256x256","ImageNet 512x512"],"metrics":["FID (Fréchet Inception Distance)"],"results":[{"metric":"FID (ImageNet 128x128)","value":"2.97"},{"metric":"FID (ImageNet 256x256)","value":"4.59"},{"metric":"FID (ImageNet 512x512)","value":"7.72"},{"metric":"FID (ImageNet 256x256, with upsampling and classifier guidance)","value":"3.94"},{"metric":"FID (ImageNet 512x512, with upsampling and classifier guidance)","value":"3.85"}],"novelty_claims":["Demonstrate that diffusion models can surpass GANs in image sample quality on challenging datasets such as ImageNet.","Introduce classifier guidance, a simple and compute-efficient method for trading off diversity and fidelity in diffusion models using classifier gradients.","Show that improved diffusion model architectures and classifier guidance together achieve state-of-the-art results on both unconditional and conditional image synthesis.","Demonstrate that classifier guidance can be combined with upsampling diffusion models for further improvements.","Achieve competitive FID scores with as few as 25 forward passes per sample, matching BigGAN-deep while maintaining better distribution coverage."]}

methods: Improved diffusion model architecture via ablation studies
Classifier guidance for conditional image synthesis (using classifier gradients to guide diffusion sampling)
Tuning classifier gradient scale to trade off diversity and fidelity
Combining classifier guidance with upsampling diffusion models

novelty_claims: Demonstrate that diffusion models can surpass GANs in image sample quality on challenging datasets such as ImageNet.
Introduce classifier guidance, a simple and compute-efficient method for trading off diversity and fidelity in diffusion models using classifier gradients.
Show that improved diffusion model architectures and classifier guidance together achieve state-of-the-art results on both unconditional and conditional image synthesis.
Demonstrate that classifier guidance can be combined with upsampling diffusion models for further improvements.
Achieve competitive FID scores with as few as 25 forward passes per sample, matching BigGAN-deep while maintaining better distribution coverage.
